findings.where: SiteHeader wordmark over hero mint background (mobile)
findings.expected: Light-tone header wordmark clearly visible against mint hero
findings.got: Wordmark 'Bodhi / SCHOOL OF YOGA' renders with low contrast over the mint hero background — readable but faint
findings.fix_suggestion: Verify SiteHeader 'light' tone token resolves to a dark-on-light color when not scrolled. Figma reference shows a darker wordmark over the mint band.

findings.expected: Italic Fraunces (per source-file comments in trainers-hero/pull-quote sections)
findings.got: Italic Host Grotesk — apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx imports Host_Grotesk as --font-heading, not Fraunces. Computed h1 font-family: 'Host Grotesk'.
findings.fix_suggestion: Either update comment headers to say 'Host Grotesk italic' OR swap the next/font import to Fraunces. Visual italic display style still reads correctly.

findings.expected: Mint-pale glyph (spec: 'giant italic Fraunces opening-quote glyph in mint')
findings.got: Mobile branch uses 'text-text-cyan/35' — a faint cyan at 35% opacity. Desktop branch correctly uses text-text-mint-pale.
findings.fix_suggestion: Change mobile span class in trainers-pull-quote-band.tsx from 'text-text-cyan/35' to 'text-text-mint-pale' to match desktop and Figma.

findings.got: Mobile branch applies 'uppercase tracking-[0.16em]' rendering as '— THE BODHI FACULTY'. Desktop branch correctly omits uppercase.
findings.fix_suggestion:
Remove 'uppercase tracking-[0.16em]' from the mobile attribution

in trainers-pull-quote-band.tsx.

findings.expected: Per user spec: 1 col mobile → 2 cols sm → 3 cols lg
findings.got: Mobile-faculty grid: 1 col mobile, 2 cols sm, hidden md+. Separate md+ grid renders 18-trainer roster with md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 xl:grid-cols-6. At lg (1440px) computed grid-template-columns = 311.094px × 4 (= 4 cols, not 3).
findings.fix_suggestion: If single-roster 1/2/3 is desired, replace md+ grid classes with 'grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3' and drop the md:hidden split. If two-roster (7 mobile / 18 desktop) is intentional, accept lg=4.

findings.got: Portrait uses aspect-[3/4] — on 358px-wide card the portrait renders ~475px tall, well above 260px
findings.fix_suggestion: Acceptable if 3:4 portrait crops match Figma reference. Otherwise fix to h-[260px] or aspect-[4/3].

findings.where: Closing CTA heading 'Begin where you are.' (mobile 390px)
findings.expected: Heading fits on at most 2 lines at 390px with italic 'you are.' inline with 'Begin where'
findings.got: At 390px the heading wraps to 3 lines: 'Begin where' / 'you' / 'are.' — the italic accent breaks across lines because text-[40px] is too large for the viewport
findings.fix_suggestion: Reduce mobile font-size in closing-cta-section.tsx h2 classes: change 'text-[40px] sm:text-[64px]' to 'text-[32px] sm:text-[56px]' (or use a wider max-width).

findings.expected: Footer block renders ONLY the dark footer (nav columns + brand block) below the page's existing mint ClosingCtaSection
findings.got: SiteFooterBlock defaults to showCta=true, so it renders its OWN dark ClosingCtaSection ('Bodhi / Begin where you are. / Try a class, free') above the footer columns. Result: TWO 'Begin where you are.' closing-CTA bands render back-to-back (mint light theme from page.tsx + dark theme from SiteFooterBlock default). Verified via DOM inspection — section[5] bg rgb(229,255,249) with 3 cards, section[6] bg rgb(0,40,44) (brand-dark) with 0 cards, both containing the same headline.
findings.fix_suggestion: apps/web/src/app/our-trainers/page.tsx line 55: change <SiteFooterBlock /> to <SiteFooterBlock showCta={false} /> to suppress the embedded duplicate CTA.

findings.got: Default href on the duplicated dark CTA is '/contact', while the page-level light CTA points to '/book'. If both were intentional the duplicate destinations would confuse users.
findings.fix_suggestion: Becomes moot once showCta={false} is applied to <SiteFooterBlock />.

closing_cta_cards.found: All three <Link> hrefs present with ArrowRight icon

duplicate_dark_cta.found: Second 'Try a class, free' Link href='/contact' from SiteFooterBlock default

desktop_1440.note: md+ uses 18-trainer roster grid (md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 xl:grid-cols-6); mobile-faculty grid hidden md+. Two-roster design pattern appears intentional per source comments — visual still matches Figma desktop reference.

sm_720.trainer_grid_columns: 2 (computed 326.797px × 2 from the still-visible mobile-faculty grid up to md)

summary: Critical duplicate closing-CTA: SiteFooterBlock renders its default dark ClosingCtaSection on top of the page's explicit light ClosingCtaSection, producing two stacked 'Begin where you are. / Try a class, free' bands at the bottom of /our-trainers. One-line fix: pass showCta={false} to <SiteFooterBlock /> in apps/web/src/app/our-trainers/page.tsx. All other findings are minor (mobile attribution uppercase, mobile glyph cyan vs mint, mobile heading 3-line wrap, lg=4 vs spec lg=3) or cosmetic (component header comments say 'Fraunces' while font-heading actually resolves to Host Grotesk). Visual fidelity of hero, pull-quote band, trainer grid, and the (intended) light closing CTA all match the Figma mobile reference closely.
